name: PACCAR INC
business: PACCAR is a global technology company whose Truck segment includes the design and manufacture of high-quality light-, medium- and heavy-duty commercial trucks. The Parts segment includes the distribution of aftermarket parts for trucks and related commercial vehicles. The Financial Services segment derives its earnings primarily from financing or leasing PACCAR products in North America, Europe, Australia and South America.
sellsTo: Dealers and retail customers in U.S. and Canada
Dealers and retail customers in Europe
Dealers and retail customers in Mexico, South America, Australia and other

exposures.sentence: The Company is embedding artificial intelligence across its business to drive innovation, profitable growth and enhanced performance for the Company's customers.

PACCAR is investing in next generation clean diesel and alternative powertrains, integrated connected vehicle services, flexible manufacturing capabilities and autonomous and advanced driver assistance systems that create value for customers.
In addition to the capital and R&D investments, the Company expects to continue investing in its U.S.-based battery joint venture, Amplify Cell Technologies.

catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, the Company issued $400.0 million of medium-term notes under this registration.

tamClaims.claim: Truck industry heavy-duty retail sales in the U.S. and Canada in 2026 are expected to be 230,000 to 270,000 units
tamClaims.sentence: Truck industry heavy-duty retail sales in the U.S. and Canada in 2026 are expected to be 230,000 to 270,000 units compared to 232,800 in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: In Europe, the 2026 truck industry registrations for over 16-tonne vehicles are expected to be 280,000 to 320,000 units
tamClaims.sentence: In Europe, the 2026 truck industry registrations for over 16-tonne vehicles are expected to be 280,000 to 320,000 units compared to 297,000 in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: In South America, heavy-duty truck industry registrations in 2026 are projected to be 100,000 to 110,000
tamClaims.sentence: In South America, heavy-duty truck industry registrations in 2026 are projected to be 100,000 to 110,000 compared to 115,000 in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Industry retail sales in the heavy-duty market in the U.S. and Canada was 232,800 units in 2025
tamClaims.sentence: In 2025, industry retail sales in the heavy-duty market in the U.S. and Canada was 232,800 units compared to 268,100 units in 2024.

tamClaims.claim: The over 16-tonne truck market in Europe in 2025 decreased to 297,000 units from 316,100 units in 2024
tamClaims.sentence: The over 16-tonne truck market in Europe in 2025 decreased to 297,000 units from 316,100 units in 2024.

tamClaims.claim: The over 16-tonne truck market in Brasil in 2025 was 86,700 units compared to 97,700 units in 2024
tamClaims.sentence: The over 16-tonne truck market in Brasil in 2025 was 86,700 units compared to 97,700 units in 2024, and DAF Brasil's market share was 8.6% in 2025 compared to 9.9% in 2024.
